scope: FQ-13P0: the foundational runtime asset & variant audit for the FQ-13P visual-consolidation arc - an audit tool and documentation that gate P1-P4. No runtime code, scene, or engine-loaded data change; no placeholder art authored yet.
summary: Before touching the visual pipeline, FQ-13P0 establishes ground truth about what art exists versus what the game actually draws. scripts/asset_audit.py scans art/generated against data/visual_assets.json and the block/item/enemy authorities and classifies every surface as LIVE, AVAILABLE_NOT_CONSUMED, PLACEHOLDER_REQUIRED, FALLBACK_ONLY, or DEFERRED. It separates informational FINDINGS from strict-failing DATA BUGS (variant sequence gaps, wrong dimensions - with width-only handling for the full-frame backgrounds and opening frames - and unreadable PNGs), so --strict guards against real regressions without failing on known deferred gaps. docs/UI_ASSET_GAPS.md is the human authority, mapping each category to its exact runtime consumer (simple_threat._draw, world._set_tile/_build_tileset, player_visual.sync_from_player, hud.item_icon, world_backdrop.layer_texture, world._make_wall_texture), recording canonical-live vs variants-live, the selection and fallback rules, the HUD sub-surfaces (orbs, slots, dock, nav buttons, cursors) as PLACEHOLDER_REQUIRED with reserved hook ids, and a 'what remains temporary' table. The headline finding validates the spec's warning that a present file is not necessarily used: the enemy variant pools (cave_crawler/raider_basic/surface_slime _01..03, nine valid 16x16 PNGs) are on disk but simple_threat._draw reads only the canonical single, so they are AVAILABLE_NOT_CONSUMED - FQ-13P1's target. The block variant mechanism is already live (per-cell posmod(hash(x,y,seed))) but has no authored _NN files, so drop-in art activates variety with no code change. Everything added since the first art pass (FQ-10 ores, FQ-11 ingots, FQ-12 crops/soil, FQ-13 enemies and drops) is FALLBACK_ONLY and safe. The run also records the player cosmetic-variation decision - a bounded full-body pool reusing the FQ-09V <id>_NN.png convention, with a character-owned visual_variant index that is presentation-only and never enters world saves, and layered composition as the documented future upgrade - and flags it for operator review before implementation. Audit state: 3 findings, 0 data bugs; suite unchanged at 283/283.
acceptance: docs/UI_ASSET_GAPS.md is a complete per-category runtime asset audit (created; it did not previously exist)
scripts/asset_audit.py reports orphaned variant pools, sequence gaps, wrong dimensions, orphaned UI/opening, and FALLBACK_ONLY gaps; manifest-entry->missing stays hard-failed in validate_repo.py
The enemy variant pools are correctly identified as AVAILABLE_NOT_CONSUMED (presence != consumption)
Player cosmetic-variation approach chosen and documented before implementation (full-body pool)
No runtime change: validate_repo PASS, asset_audit --strict exit 0, capsule doctor healthy, smoke 283/283 unchanged, git diff --check clean
validation: python scripts/asset_audit.py --strict -> exit 0 (3 findings, 0 data bugs)
COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 waited headless run -> 283/283 PASS (unchanged; no runtime change)
git diff --check -> 0 whitespace errors (LF->CRLF notices only)
decisions: Execute only FQ-13P0 (audit + tool + decision) this run; the spec requires the audit and the player-variation choice before any P1-P4 implementation

asset_audit.py is report-only and standalone; it separates informational FINDINGS from strict-failing DATA BUGS, and manifest->missing stays the hard fail in validate_repo.py rather than duplicating it

Reserved UI hook ids live centrally in asset_audit.py + the audit doc, NOT yet in visual_assets.json, because the manifest validator requires every entry to map to an existing file (they are added when the first placeholder PNG is authored)

Player cosmetic variation uses the bounded full-body pool (reuse FQ-09V <id>_NN.png; character-owned, never-saved visual_variant index), with layered composition as the documented future upgrade - flagged for operator review
AVAILABLE_NOT_CONSUMED (e.g. enemy pools) is an informational finding, not a build failure - it is a known deferred state fixed in P1
Self-review only (no agent spawned) for a documentation/tooling increment with no runtime change
risks: The 'runtime consumer' / 'variants live' columns are hand-verified against code, not machine-derived; update the doc and asset_audit.py's consumer sets together when a consumer changes
asset_audit.py --strict is a separate validation step, not yet wired into validate_repo.py
The player-variation decision is recorded but unbuilt and awaits operator sign-off; FQ-13P1 (enemy pools) does not depend on it
next_action: FQ-13P1: consume enemy variant pools with a deterministic, lifetime-stable per-enemy selection plus smoke coverage.
